channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office
item.description: Thomas Shedd, the commissioner of Federal Acquisition Service, said the decision is part of the President’s Workforce Optimization Initiative executive order. The post After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Samsung Galaxy tri-fold – 5 key rumored features, from its screen size to its hinge
item.description: Samsung has only hinted at the existence of a tri-fold Galaxy phone, but that hasn’t stopped tipsters and leakers from providing us with a wealth of rumors about the supposed new addition to the Samsung lineup.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: DOGE isn’t Silicon Valley innovation—it’s just a sloppy rebrand of free-market dogma
item.description: At a press conference in the Oval Office earlier this month, Elon Musk—a billionaire who is not, at least formally, the President of the United States—was asked how the Department of Government Efficiency manages potential conflicts of interest to ensure “accountability and transparency.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: TrapC proposal to fix C/C++ memory safety
item.description: TrapC, a fork of the C language, is being developed as a potential solution for memory safety issues that have hindered the C and C++ languages. TrapC is a proposed C-language extension compiler intended to be implemented as a cybersecurity compiler for C and C++ code, said developer Robin Rowe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Alexa+ – Here’s how to sign up for early access
item.description: Amazon's now letting customers in the United States sign up for early access to Alexa+. Here's how to do just that and the Echo Show device you'll need to be eligible.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: This is probably the best Windows alternative to the M4 Mac Mini: AMD-powered mini PC can drive four 8K monitors and has two 2.5Gb Ethernet connectors
item.description: The AuBox 8745 mini PC from Chuwi could rival the Apple M4 Mac Mini - but how does it shape up in terms of performance?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: East Bay startup’s new stove that plugs into a normal wall outlet could be major gain for health and climate
item.description: The sleek, standard four-burner electric induction stove runs on 120 volts, meaning there is no need to pay a licensed electrician thousands of dollars to rewire to 240 volts, which many electric stoves require.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Artificial Intelligence Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2025
item.description: How are agencies refining their AI strategy? The post Federal Executive Forum Artificial Intelligence Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Skype to shut 14 years after Microsoft’s $8.5 billion purchase
item.description: Once a byword for digital calls that bypassed long-distance charges, Skype was surpassed in recent years by smartphone-native communication apps and Zoom video calls.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: What is Apple Intelligence: everything you need to know about the AI toolkit
item.description: Apple Intelligence is a built-in AI toolkit for iPhone, iPad and Mac users. It’s designed to make life easier through suggestions, automations and personalization. Here’s why it’s so useful.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to get Novacrystal in Monster Hunter Wilds
item.description: Here's where to find Novacrystal in Monster Hunter Wilds, so that you can start crafting better armor sets and weapons.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tech, life science companies slash hundreds more Bay Area workers
item.description: Latest layoffs affect workers in the South Bay, East Bay and SF, marking a fresh round of employment setbacks for the vital sectors.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Why fast-learning robots are wearing Meta glasses
item.description: AI and robots need data — lots of it. Companies that have millions of users have an advantage in this data collection, because they can use the data of their customers. A well-known example is Google’s reCAPTCHA v2 service, which I recently lambasted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI priorities: IT leaders emphasize productivity over innovation
item.description: IT leaders are still aiming for the low-hanging fruit when using AI, zeroing in on improving employee productivity rather than more advanced uses such as enabling innovation or expanding revenue, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 11 rules for writing better code
item.description: You can’t develop software and manage software projects for 30 years and not learn a thing or two or twelve about writing good code. Here are a near-dozen nuggets of coding wisdom I picked up along the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reported cuts at NIST imperil semiconductor reshoring plans in the US
item.description: Proposed cuts at a key US government agency will likely mean the demise of CHIPS Act funding, which could effectively end the program and undermine efforts to reshore semiconductor manufacturing and research to the US.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco, Nvidia expand AI partnership to include Silicon One technology
item.description: Cisco and Nvidia have expanded their collaboration to support enterprise AI implementations by tying together Cisco’s Silicon One technology and Nvidia’s Ethernet networking platform.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IBM targets AI application growth with DataStax buy
item.description: Looking to bolster its AI data handling and storage capabilities, IBM said it had entered an agreement to buy open-source database developer DataStax for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Precise asset management leads to impactful zero trust initiatives
item.description: Agencies’ attention and efforts are now shifting towards strengthening cybersecurity defenses to address the more challenging aspects of cyber threats. The post Precise asset management leads to impactful zero trust initiatives first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Connolly calls on OPM to reverse CIO classification change
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly, the ranking member of the Oversight and Accountability Committee, said the CIOs should remain career-reserve SES positions. The post Connolly calls on OPM to reverse CIO classification change first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal News Network’s DoD Modernization Exchange 2025
item.description: Hear from top Defense and industry leaders on the latest tech, strategy and innovation shaping DoD modernization The post Federal News Network’s DoD Modernization Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4
